'''Morgan Gannon''' (born 2 December 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Gannon was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax]], [[West Yorkshire, England]]. His brother Jacob Gannon played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and they are both sons of former player [[Jim Gannon (rugby league)|Jim Gannon]].

He played his junior rugby league for Siddal.<ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/it-was-surreal-teenager-morgan-gannon-delighted-to-make-leeds-rhinos-debut-3197415 'It was surreal' - teenager Morgan Gannon delighted to make Leeds Rhinos debut]</ref>

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
In 2021 he made professional début for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] against [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[2021 Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3564241|title=St Helens v Leeds Rhinos|work=BBC|date=10 April 2021|accessdate=15 April 2021}}</ref>

===New Zealand Warriors===
On 26 April 2025, Gannon signed a three-year contract beginning in [[2026 NRL season|2026]] with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] of the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2025/04/26/leeds-forward-gannon-signed/|title=Leeds back rower Gannon signed for three seasons|date=26 April 2025|access-date= 21 June 2025|author=Becht, Richard|newspaper=[[New Zealand Warriors]]}}</ref>

Gannon made his debut in a 38–12 win over the [[Newcastle Knights]] on 21 March 2026 in [[Newcastle, New South Wales|Newcastle]], Australia.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=21 Mar 2026|first=Tom |last=Smith|title=Morgan Gannon suffers head injury on New Zealand Warriors debut|url=https://www.totalrl.com/morgan-gannon-suffers-head-injury-on-new-zealand-warriors-debut/|access-date=22 March 2026}}</ref>
